previous_statement: (Applying the main bound). Under m affine constraints Tr(a_j ρ) = β_j (a_j ∈ H_n self-adjoint): the multiplicity bound gives r² = Σ n_j² ≤ m + 1 (since k = 1, n_1 = r). Hence rank(ρ) = r ≤ √(m+1).
new_statement: (Applying the main bound). Let C = {rho in S(M_n(C)) : Tr(a_j rho) = beta_j, j=1,...,m} where a_j in H_n are self-adjoint. Suppose rho is an EXTREME POINT of C. By Node 1.6.1, states on M_n(C) are density matrices and phi_rho(a) = Tr(rho a). By Node 1.6.2, M_n(C) has exactly one irreducible representation (k=1). By Node 1.6.3, for rank(rho) = r the GNS representation gives pi_rho = id^{+r}, so n_1 = r, and the commutant satisfies dim_R(pi_rho(M_n(C))'^{sa}) = r^2. By Node 1.3 (Main Dimension Bound) applied to this extreme point, r^2 = Sigma_{j=1}^k n_j^2 <= m+1 (with k=1, n_1=r). Hence rank(rho) = r <= sqrt(m+1). [Inference: modus_ponens. Dependencies: 1.3, 1.4, 1.6.1, 1.6.2, 1.6.3.]
